examples.title: Action button
examples.description: Default action dismisses after firing; keepOpen: true keeps the toast visible after the action runs.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}

Undo fired: {undoCount} · Pause fired: {pauseCount}

{/snippet}

examples.title: Basic toast region
examples.description: Click the trigger to dispatch a transient toast into the live region.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}
{/snippet}

examples.title: Dismissible sticky toast
examples.description: duration: 0 keeps the toast until the user dismisses it.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}
{/snippet}

examples.title: Keyboard dismiss
examples.description: Focus a toast control and press Escape to dismiss the active notification.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Button } from '@lostgradient/cinder/button';
  import { ToastRegion, useToast } from '@lostgradient/cinder/toast-region';
</script>

<ToastRegion>
  {#snippet children()}
    {@const toast = useToast()}
    <Button
      label="Show keyboard toast"
      onclick={() =>
        toast.show('Tab to this toast, then press Escape.', {
          duration: 0,
          action: {
            label: 'Action',
            keepOpen: true,
            onAction: () => {},
          },
        })}
    />
  {/snippet}
</ToastRegion>

examples.title: Modal-scoped region
examples.description: Nest <ToastRegion> inside a Modal to scope toasts to the modal content. When the modal body unmounts on close, the region tears down with it and pending timers are cleared.

examples.title: Pause on hover
examples.description: Pointer hover and keyboard focus pause auto-dismiss for the whole stack.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Button } from '@lostgradient/cinder/button';
  import { ToastRegion, useToast } from '@lostgradient/cinder/toast-region';
</script>

<ToastRegion defaultDuration={2500}>
  {#snippet children()}
    {@const toast = useToast()}
    <Button
      label="Show timed toast"
      onclick={() =>
        toast.show('Hover or focus the stack to pause this countdown.', {
          action: {
            label: 'Keep',
            keepOpen: true,
            onAction: () => {},
          },
        })}
    />
  {/snippet}
</ToastRegion>

examples.title: Positions
examples.description: Anchor the toast region to any viewport corner or horizontal center.

examples.title: Programmatic dismiss
examples.description: show() returns the toast id. Capture it to dismiss a specific toast, or call dismissAll() to clear both stacks.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}
{/snippet}

examples.title: Promise
examples.description: Show a sticky loading toast that resolves into success or danger output.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}
{/snippet}

examples.title: Stacking
examples.description: Rapid notifications stack with bounded overflow and visual depth.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Button } from '@lostgradient/cinder/button';
  import { ToastRegion, useToast } from '@lostgradient/cinder/toast-region';

  let count = $state(0);
</script>

<ToastRegion maxStack={5} defaultDuration={0}>
  {#snippet children()}
    {@const toast = useToast()}
    <Button
      label="Add five toasts"
      onclick={() => {
        for (let index = 0; index < 5; index += 1) {
          count += 1;
          toast.show(`Stacked notification ${count}`, {
            variant: index % 2 === 0 ? 'info' : 'success',
          });
        }
      }}
    />
  {/snippet}
</ToastRegion>

examples.title: Swipe to dismiss
examples.description: Dismiss a toast by dragging it horizontally past the threshold.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { Button } from '@lostgradient/cinder/button';
  import { ToastRegion, useToast } from '@lostgradient/cinder/toast-region';
</script>

<ToastRegion>
  {#snippet children()}
    {@const toast = useToast()}
    <Button
      label="Show swipe toast"
      onclick={() =>
        toast.show('Drag this notification left or right to dismiss it.', {
          duration: 0,
        })}
    />
  {/snippet}
</ToastRegion>

examples.title: All four variants
examples.description: info and success route to the polite (status) region; warning and danger route to the assertive (alert) region.
examples.code:
{#snippet children()} {@const toast = useToast()}
{/snippet}
